title: Iran’s Battered Leaders Emerge From War Confident — and With New Cards
summary: For Iran’s theocratic rulers, just surviving the U.S.-Israeli onslaught means victory. But the seeds of their next crisis may already be planted.

title: European Leaders Demand That U.S. Cease-Fire With Iran Include Lebanon
summary: Britain, France and the European Union condemned Israel’s strikes targeting Hezbollah in the country, saying these threatened the truce.

title: How Trump Purged Immigration Judges to Speed Up Deportations
summary: Judges are ordering an unprecedented number of people deported after coming under significant pressure from the administration to do so or risk losing their jobs.

title: In a Deep Red Town In Pennsylvania, Locals Vent Over a Planned ICE Detention Center
summary: The Tremont, Pa., area has roughly 2,000 residents and limited resources. The Trump administration plans to convert a warehouse there to hold nearly four times as many people.

title: For Artemis II, Returning to Earth May Be the Most Dangerous Part of the Mission
summary: After a successful flight around the moon, the astronauts are relying on a flawed heat shield to protect them as they re-enter Earth’s atmosphere.

title: Trump Officials Push Allies to Pursue Antifa and Far Left as Terrorist Threats
summary: The Trump administration aims to deploy counterterrorism tools against far-left groups, even as it has offered little evidence they present a dire threat.

title: 5 Takeaways From the U.S. Push Against the Far Left
summary: The Trump administration has made countering antifa and other far-left groups a counterterrorism priority, despite increasing threats from the Middle East.

title: Top C.D.C. Official Delays Report on Covid Shot’s Effectiveness
summary: Dr. Jay Bhattacharya objected to the study’s methodology, saying it gave an inaccurate picture of the vaccine’s benefits.

title: F.B.I. Arrests Ex-Fort Bragg Employee Accused of Leaking Classified Information to Journalist
summary: Courtney Williams, who worked at Fort Bragg in North Carolina, was accused of leaking classified information to a reporter.

title: Vegetative Patients May Be More Aware Than We Knew
summary: New research is upending what we thought about the consciousness of patients, leaving families with agonizing choices.

title: On Social Media, Democratic Politicians Are Letting the F-Word Fly
summary: Democratic politicians are swearing with glee. It is usually aimed at President Trump.

title: America’s Furniture Stores Struggle to Survive a Frozen Housing Market
summary: Retailers are going bankrupt and liquidating as record-low housing turnover leaves fewer customers looking to furnish homes.

title: How a New York Times Reporter Covers the Illegal Drug Trade
summary: A new series on dangerous synthetic drugs called for sources on the other side of the law.

title: The First Race of the L.A. Olympics: Buying Tickets
summary: The winners of a lottery for a presale prioritizing locals often came away with sticker shock. Still, organizers said early sales had “significantly exceeded” those of other Games.

title: MoMA Survey Shows How Marcel Duchamp Changed the Art Game
summary: Marcel Duchamp flipped the notion of art’s value on its head. We need foundation-shaking badly today, our critic says, and a sweeping survey at MoMA is an arresting reminder.

title: Melissa Chiu Exits Hirshhorn Museum to Lead Guggenheim
summary: Melissa Chiu is stepping down as director of the Hirshhorn Museum in Washington to lead the Guggenheim Museum in New York.

title: Getty Center in Los Angeles Is Closing for Year of Renovations
summary: The art museum will close to the public in March 2027 to replace its aging tram system and modernize some galleries.

title: These Homesteaders Prep for the End of the World at Oklahoma Expo
summary: Homesteading, for all its bucolic imagery, taps into the desire to escape from the disquiet of modern America, where anything can happen.

title: How Ben Sasse Is Living Now That He Is Dying
summary: The former senator wants to heal the America he’s leaving behind.

title: We Are on the Cusp of a Revolution in Rare Disease Treatment
summary: We can’t let the most important medical achievement of a decade slip through our fingers.

title: Supreme Court Secrecy Includes Reasons for Recusal
summary: Justices often don’t disclose why they disqualify themselves from hearing cases. Their silence echoes the court’s unexplained emergency orders.

title: U.S. Fertility Rates Drop to Another Record Low
summary: The fertility rate has been falling since 2007, in large part because of a plunge among teenagers.

title: 137-Year-Old Piece of Eiffel Tower to Be Auctioned in Paris
summary: A section of the landmark’s original staircase will go up for auction next month and could sell for a towering sum.
